author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter VI. 15–44.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Following upon yesterday’s lesson from the Gospel is that of to-day, upon which this day’s discourse is due to you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When that miracle was wrought, in which Jesus fed the five thousand with five loaves, and the multitudes marveled and said that He was a great prophet that came into the world, then follows this: “When Jesus therefore knew that they came to seize Him, and to make Him king, He escaped again unto the mountain alone.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is therefore given to be understood that the Lord, when He sat on the mountain with His disciples, and saw the multitudes coming to Him, had descended from the mountain, and fed the multitudes on its lower parts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how can it be that He should escape thither again, if He had not before descended from the mountain?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is something meant by the Lord’s descending from on high to feed the multitudes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He fed them, and ascended.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why did He ascend after He knew that they wished to seize Him and make Him a king?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How then; was He not a king, that He was afraid to be made a king?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was certainly not such a king as would be made by men, but such as would bestow a kingdom on men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May it not be that Jesus, whose deeds are words, does here, too, signify some thing to us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore in this, that they wished to seize Him and make Him a king, and that for this He escapes to the mountain alone, is this action in His case silent; does it speak nothing, does it mean nothing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or was this seizing of Him perhaps an intention to anticipate the time of His kingdom?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He had come now, not to reign immediately, as He is to reign in the sense in which we pray, Thy kingdom come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He ever reigns, indeed, with the Father, in that He is the Son of God, the Word of God, the Word by which all things were made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the prophets foretold His kingdom according to that wherein He is Christ made man, and has made His faithful ones Christians.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There will consequently be a kingdom of Christians, which at present is being gathered together, being prepared and purchased by the blood of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His kingdom will at length be made manifest, when the glory of His saints shall be revealed, after the judgment is executed by Him, which judgment He Himself has said above is that which the Son of man shall execute.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of which kingdom also the apostle has said: “When He shall have delivered up the kingdom to God, even the Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In reference to which also Himself says: “Come, ye blessed of my Father, receive the kingdom which is prepared for you from the beginning of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the disciples and the multitudes that believed on Him thought that He had thus come immediately to reign; hence, they wished to seize Him and to make Him a king; they wished to anticipate the time which He hid with Himself, to make it known in due time, and in due time to declare it in the end of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That ye may know that they wished to make Him a king,—that is, to anticipate, and at once to have manifest the kingdom of Christ, whom it behoved first to be judged and then to judge,—when He was crucified, and they who hoped in Him had lost hope of His resurrection, having risen from the dead, He found two of them despairingly conversing together, and, with groaning, talking with one another of what had been done; and appearing to them as a stranger, while their eyes were held that He should not be recognized by them, He mixed with them as they held discourse: but they, narrating to Him the matter of their conversation, said that He was a prophet, mighty in deeds and in words, that had been slain by the chief priests; “And we,” say they, “did hope that it was He that should have redeemed Israel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rightly you hoped: a true thing you hoped for: in Him is the redemption of Israel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why are ye in haste?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye wish to seize it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The following, too, shows us that this was their feeling, that, when the disciples inquired of Him concerning the end, they said to Him, “Wilt Thou at this time be made manifest, and when will be the kingdom of Israel?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they longed for it now, they wished it now; that is, they wished to seize Him, and to make Him king.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But saith He to the disciples (for He had yet to ascend alone), “It is not for you to know the times or seasons which the Father hath put in His own power: but ye shall receive virtue from on high, the Holy Spirit coming upon you, and ye shall be witnesses to me in Jerusalem, and in all Judea and Samaria, and unto the ends of the earth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You wish that I should manifest the kingdom now; let me first gather what I may manifest; you love elevation, and you shall obtain elevation, but follow me through humility.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus it was also foretold of Him, “And the gathering of the peoples will surround Thee, and for this cause return Thou on high;” that is, that the gatherings of the peoples may surround Thee, that Thou mayest gather many together, return Thou on high.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus He did; He fed men, and ascended.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why is it said, He escaped?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He could not be held against His will, nor seized against His will, since He could not be recognized against His will.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that you may know that this was done mystically, not of necessity, but of express purpose, you will presently see in the following: that He appeared to the same multitudes that sought Him, said many things in speaking with them, and discoursed much about the bread of heaven; when discoursing about bread, was He not with the same people from whom He had escaped lest He should be held of them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then, could He not have so acted at that time that He should not be seized by them, just as afterwards when He was speaking with them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Something, therefore, was meant by His escaping.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means, He escaped?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His loftiness could not be understood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For of anything which thou hast not understood thou sayest, “It has escaped me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, “He escaped again unto the mountain alone,—the first-begotten from the dead, ascending above all heavens, and interceding for us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Meanwhile, He, the one great High Priest being above (He who has entered into that within the veil, the people standing without; for Him that priest under the old law, who did this once a year, did signify): He then be ing above, what were the disciples enduring in the ship?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that ship prefigured the Church while He is on high.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if we do not, in the first place, understand this thing which that ship suffered respecting the Church, those incidents were not significant, but simply transient; but if we see the real meaning of those signs expressed in the Church, it is manifest that the actions of Christ are a kind of speeches.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But when it was late, saith he, His disciples went down to the sea; and when they had entered into a ship, they came over the sea to Capernaum.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He declared that as finished quickly, which was done afterwards,—“They came over the sea to Capernaum.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He returns to explain how they came; that they passed over by sailing across the lake.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And whilst they were sailing to that place to which He has already said they had come, He explains by recapitulation what befell them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “It was now dark, and Jesus had not come to them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rightly he said “dark,” for the light had not come to them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “It was now dark, and Jesus had not come to them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As the end of the world approaches, errors increase, terrors multiply, iniquity increases, infidelity increases; the light, in short, which, by the Evangelist John himself, is fully and clearly shown to be charity, so much so that he says, “Whoso hateth his brother is in darkness;” that light, I say, is very often extinguished; this darkness of enmity between brethren increases, daily increases, and Jesus is not yet come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How does it appear to increase?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because iniquity will abound, and the love of many will begin to wax cold.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Darkness increases, and Jesus is not yet come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Darkness increasing, love waxing cold, iniquity abounding,—these are the waves that agitate the ship; the storms and the winds are the clamors of revilers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thence love waxes cold; thence the waves do swell, and the ship is tossed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And a great wind blowing, the sea rose.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Darkness was increasing, discernment was diminishing, iniquity was growing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “When, therefore, they had rowed about twenty-five or thirty furlongs.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Meanwhile they struggled onward, kept advancing; nor did those winds and storms, and waves and darkness effect either that the ship should not make way, or that it should break in pieces and founder; but amid all these evils it went on.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, notwithstanding iniquity abounds, and the love of many waxes cold, and the waves do swell, the darkness grows and the wind rages, yet the ship is moving forward; “for he that perseveres to the end, the same shall be saved.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor is that number of furlongs to be lightly regarded.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it cannot really be that nothing is meant, when it is said that, “when they had rowed twenty-five or thirty furlongs, Jesus came to them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It were enough to say, “twenty-five,” so likewise “thirty;” especially as it was an estimate, not an assertion of the narrator.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Could the truth be aught endangered by a mere estimate, if he had said nearly thirty furlongs, or nearly twenty-five furlongs?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But from twenty-five he made thirty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us examine the number twenty-five.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of what does it consist? of what is it made up?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of the quinary, or number five.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That number five pertains to the law.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The same are the five books of Moses, the same are those five porches containing the sick folk, the same are the five loaves feeding the five thousand men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly the number twenty-five signifies the law, because five by five—that is, five times five—make twenty-five, or the number five squared.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this law lacked perfection before the gospel came.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, perfection is comprised in the number six.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore in six days God finished, or perfected, the world, and the same five are multiplied by six, that the law may be completed by the gospel, that six times five become thirty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To them that fulfill the law, therefore, Jesus comes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how does He come?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Walking upon the waves, keeping all the swellings of the world under His feet, pressing down all its heights.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus it goes on, so long as time endures, so long as the ages roll.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Tribulations increase, calamities increase, sorrows increase, all these swell and mount up: Jesus passeth on treading upon the waves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet so great are the tribulations, that even they who have trusted in Jesus, and who strive to persevere unto the end, greatly fear lest they fail; while Christ is treading the waves, and trampling down the world’s ambitions and heights, the Christian is sorely afraid.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Were not these things foretold him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Justly “they were afraid,” too, at seeing Jesus walking on the waves; like as Christians, though having hope in the world to come, are frequently disquieted at the crash of human affairs, when they see the loftiness of this world trampled down.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They open the Gospel, they open the Scriptures, and they find all these things there foretold; that this is the Lord’s doing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He tramples down the heights of the world, that He may be glorified by the humble.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Concerning whose loftiness it is foretold: “Thou shalt destroy strongest cities,” and “the spears of the enemy have come to an end, and Thou hast destroyed cities.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why then are ye afraid, O Christians?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ speaks: “It is I; be not afraid.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why are ye alarmed at these things?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why are ye afraid?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have foretold these things, I do them, they must necessarily be done.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “It is I; be not afraid.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore they would receive Him into the ship.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Recognizing Him and rejoicing, they are freed from their fears.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And immediately the ship was at the land to which they went.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is an end made at the land; from the watery to the solid, from the agitated to the firm, from the way to the goal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “On the next day the multitude that stood on the other side of the sea,” whence the disciples had come, “saw that there was none other boat there, save that one whereinto His disciples were entered, and that Jesus went not with His disciples into the boat, but that His disciples were gone away alone; but there came other boats from Tiberias, nigh unto the place where they did eat bread, giving thanks to the Lord: when, therefore, the multitudes saw that Jesus was not there, nor His disciples, they also took shipping, and came to Capernaum seeking Jesus.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet they got some knowledge of so great a miracle.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they saw that the disciples had gone into the ship alone, and that there was not another ship there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But there came boats also from near to that place where they did eat bread; in these the multitudes followed Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had not then embarked with His disciples, and there was not another ship there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, was Jesus on a sudden beyond the sea, unless that He walked upon the sea to show a miracle?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And when the multitudes had found Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, He presents Himself to the people from whom He had escaped into the mountain, afraid that He should be taken of them by force.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In every way He proves to us and gives us to know that all these things are said in a mystery, and done in a great sacrament (or mystery) to signify something important.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, that is He who had escaped the crowds unto the mountain; is He not speaking with the same crowds?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them hold Him now; let them now make Him a king.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And when they had found Him on the other side of the sea, they said unto Him, Rabbi, when camest Thou hither?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: After the sacrament of the miracle, He introduces discourse, that, if possible, they who have been fed may be further fed, that He may with discourse fill their minds, whose bellies He filled with the loaves, provided they take in.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if they do not, let that be taken up which they do not receive, that the fragments may not be lost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore let Him speak, and let us hear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus answered and said Verily, verily, I say unto you, ye seek me, not because ye saw the signs, but because ye have eaten of my loaves.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye seek me for the sake of the flesh not for the sake of the spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How many seek Jesus for no other object but that He may bestow on them a temporal benefit!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One has a business on hand, he seeks the intercession of the clergy; another is oppressed by one more powerful than himself, he flies to the church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Another desires intervention in his behalf with one with whom he has little influence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One in this way, one in that, the church is daily filled with such people.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus is scarcely sought after for Jesus’ sake.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Ye seek me, not because ye have seen the signs, but because ye have eaten of my loaves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Labor not for the meat which perisheth, but for that which endureth unto eternal life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye seek me for something else, seek me for my own sake.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He insinuates the truth, that Himself is that meat: this shines out clearly in the sequel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Which the Son of man will give you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou didst expect, I believe, again to eat bread, again to sit down, again to be gorged.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He had said, “Not the meat which perisheth, but that which endureth unto eternal life,” in the same manner as it was said to that Samaritan woman: “If thou knewest who it is that asketh of thee drink, thou wouldest perhaps have asked of Him, and He would give thee living water.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When she said, “Whence hast thou, since thou hast nothing to draw with, and the well is deep?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He answered the Samaritan woman: “If thou knewest who it is that asketh of thee drink, thou wouldst have asked of Him, and He would give thee water, whereof whoso drinketh shall thirst no more; for whoso drinketh of this water shall thirst again.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And she was glad and would receive, as if no more to suffer thirst of body, being wearied with the labor of drawing water.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so, during a conversation of this kind, He comes to spiritual drink.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Entirely in this manner also here.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore “this meat, not that which perisheth, but that which endureth unto everlasting life, which the Son of man shall give unto you; for Him hath God the Father sealed.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not take this Son of man as you take other sons of men, of whom it is said, “And the sons of men will trust in the protection of Thy wings.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This Son of man is separated by a certain grace of the spirit; Son of man according to the flesh, taken out from the number of men: He is the Son of man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This Son of man is also the Son of God; this man is even God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In another place, when questioning His disciples, He saith: “Whom do men say that I, the Son of man, am?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And they answered, Some John, some Elias, some Jeremias, or one of the prophets.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He said unto them, But whom say ye that I am?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Peter answered, Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He declared Himself Son of man, Peter declared Him the Son of the living God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Most fitly did He mention that which in mercy He had manifested Himself to be; most fitly did the other mention that which He continues to be in glory.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Word of God commends to our attention His own humility: the man acknowledged the glory of his Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And indeed, brethren, I think that this is just.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He humbled Himself for us, let us glorify Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For not for Himself is He Son of man, but for us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore was He Son of man in that way, when “the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For to that end “God the Father sealed Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is to seal, but to put some particular mark?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To seal is to impress some mark which cannot be confounded with the rest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To seal is to put a mark on a thing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When thou puttest a mark on anything, thou doest so lest it might be confused with other things, and thou shouldst not be able to recognize it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Father,” then, “hath sealed Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is that, “hath sealed”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Bestowed on Him something peculiar, which puts Him out of comparison with all other men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that reason it is said of Him, “God, even Thy God, hath anointed Thee with the oil of gladness above Thy fellows.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is it then to seal, but to have Him excepted?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is the import of “above Thy fellows.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so, do not, saith He, despise me because I am the Son of man, but seek from me, “not the meat that perisheth, but that which endureth to eternal life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I am the Son of man in such manner as not to be one of you: I am Son of man in such manner that God the Father sealed me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does that mean, He “sealed me”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Gave me something peculiarly my own, that I should not be confounded with mankind, but that mankind should be delivered by me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They said therefore unto Him, What shall we do, that we may work the works of God?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He had said to them, “Labor not for the meat which perisheth, but for that which endureth unto eternal life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “What shall we do?” they ask; by observing what, shall we be able to fulfill this precept?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus answered and said unto them, This is the work of God, that ye believe on Him whom He has sent.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is then to eat the meat, not that which perisheth, but that which endureth unto eternal life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To what purpose dost thou make ready teeth and stomach?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Believe, and thou hast eaten already.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Faith is indeed distinguished from works, even as the apostle says, “that a man is justified by faith without the works of the law:” there are works which appear good, without faith in Christ; but they are not good, because they are not referred to that end in which works are good; “for Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to every one that believeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that reason, He willeth not to distinguish faith from work, but declared faith itself to be work.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is that same faith that worketh by love.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor did He say, This is your work; but, “This is the work of God, that ye believe on Him whom He has sent;” so that he who glories, may glory in the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And because He invited them to faith, they, on the other hand, were still asking for signs by which they might believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See if the Jews do not ask for signs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They said therefore unto Him, What sign doest thou, that we may see and believe thee? what dost thou work?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it a trifle that they were fed with five loaves?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They knew this indeed, but they preferred manna from heaven to this food.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Lord Jesus declared Himself to be such an one, that He was superior to Moses.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Moses dared not say of himself that ge gave, “not the meat which perisheth, but that which endureth to eternal life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus promised something greater than Moses gave.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By Moses indeed was promised a kingdom, and a land flowing with milk and honey, temporal peace, abundance of children, health of body, and all other things, temporal goods indeed, yet in figure spiritual; because in the Old Testament they were promised to the old man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They considered therefore the things promised by Moses, and they considered the things promised by Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The former promised a full belly on the earth, but of the meat which perisheth; the latter promised, “not the meat which perisheth, but that which endureth unto eternal life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They gave attention to Him that promised the more, but just as if they did not yet see Him do greater things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They considered therefore what sort of works Moses had done, and they wished yet some greater works to be done by Him who promised them such great things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, say they, doest thou, that we may believe thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that thou mayest know that they compared those former miracles with this and so judged these miracles which Jesus did as being less; “Our fathers,” say they, “did eat manna in the wilderness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what is manna?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps ye despise it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “As it is written, He gave them manna to eat.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By Moses our fathers received bread from heaven, and Moses did not say to them, “Labor for the meat which perisheth not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou promisest “meat which perisheth not, but which endureth to eternal life;” and yet thou workest not such works as Moses did.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He gave, not barley loaves, but manna from heaven.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, not Moses gave you bread from heaven, but my Father gave you bread from heaven.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the true bread is He that cometh down from heaven, and giveth life to the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The true bread then is He that giveth life to the world; and the same is the meat of which I have spoken a little before,—“Labor not for the meat which perisheth, but for that which endureth unto eternal life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, both that manna signified this meat, and all those signs were signs of me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye have longed for signs of me; do ye despise Him that was signified?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not Moses then gave bread from heaven: God gives bread.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what bread?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Manna, perhaps?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No, but the bread which manna signified, namely, the Lord Jesus Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: My Father giveth you the true bread.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For the bread of God is He that cometh down from heaven, and giveth life to the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then said they unto Him, Lord, evermore give us this bread.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Like that Samaritan woman, to whom it was said, “Whoso drinketh of this water shall never thirst.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She, immediately understanding it in reference to the body, and wishing to be rid of want, said, “Give me, O Lord, of this water;” in the same manner also these said, “O Lord, give us this bread;” which may refresh us, and yet not fail.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And Jesus said unto them, I am the Bread of Life: he that cometh to me shall never hunger; and he that believeth on me shall never thirst.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that cometh to me;” this is the same thing as “He that believeth on me;” and “shall never hunger” is to be understood to mean the same thing as “shall never thirst.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For by both is signified that eternal sufficiency in which there is no want.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You desire bread from heaven; you have it before you, and yet you do not eat.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But I said unto you, that ye also have seen me, and ye believed not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But I have not on that account lost my people.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For hath your unbelief made the faith of God of none effect?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, see thou what follows: “All that the Father giveth me shall come to me; and him that cometh to me, I will not cast out of doors.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What kind of within is that, whence there is no going out of doors?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Noble interior, sweet retreat!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O secret dwelling without weariness, without the bitterness of evil thoughts, without the solicitings of temptations and the interruptions of griefs!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it not that secret dwelling whither shall enter that well-deserving servant, to whom the Lord will say, “Enter thou into the joy of thy Lord?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And him that will come to me, I will not cast out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I came down from heaven, not to do mine own will, but the will of Him that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it for that reason that Thou wilt not cast out him that shall come unto Thee, because Thou hast descended from heaven, not to do Thine own will, but the will of Him that sent Thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Great mystery!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I beseech you, let us knock together; something may come forth to us which may feed us, according to that which has delighted us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That great and sweet secret dwelling-place: “He that will come to me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give heed, give heed, and weigh the matter: “He that will come unto me, I will not cast out.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because I came down from heaven, not to do my own will, but the will of Him that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it then the very reason why Thou castest not out him that cometh unto Thee, that Thou camest down from heaven, not to do Thy own will, but the will of Him that sent Thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The very reason.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why do we ask whether it be the same?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The same it is; Himself says it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it would not be right in us to suspect Him to mean other than He says, “Whoso will come to me, I will not cast out.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And, as if thou askedst, wherefore?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He answered, “Because I came not to do my own will, but the will of Him that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am afraid that the reason why the soul went forth away from God is, that it was proud; nay, I do not doubt it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is written, “Pride is the beginning of all sin; and the beginning of man’s pride is a falling away from God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is written, it is firm and sure, it is true.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And hence what is said of proud mortal man, clad in the tattered rags of the flesh, weighed down with the weight of a corruptible body, and withal extolling himself, and forgetting with what skin-coat he is clothed,—what, I ask, saith the Scripture to him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Why is dust and ashes proud?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the Scripture tell why.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because in his life he put forth his inmost parts.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is “put forth,” but “threw afar off”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is to go forth away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For to enter within, is to long after the inmost parts; to put forth the inmost parts, is to go forth away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The proud man puts forth the inmost parts, the humble man earnestly desires the inmost parts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we are cast out by pride, let us return by humility.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Pride is the source of all diseases, because pride is the source of all sins.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When a physician removes a disorder from the body, if he merely cures the malady produced by some particular cause, but not the cause itself, he seems to heal the patient for a time, but while the cause remains, the disease will repeat itself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For example, to speak of this more expressly, some humor in the body produces a scurf or sores; there follows a high fever, and not a little pain; certain remedies are applied to repress the scurf, and to allay that heat of the sore; the remedies are applied, and they do good; thou seest the man who was full of sores and scurf healed; but because that humor was not expelled, it returns again to ulcers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The physician, perceiving this, purges away the humor, removes the cause, and there will be no more sores.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence doth iniquity abound?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Cure pride and there will be no more iniquity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consequently, that the cause of all diseases might be cured, namely, pride, the Son of God came down and was made low.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why art thou proud, O man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God, for thee, became low.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou wouldst perhaps be ashamed to imitate a lowly man; at any rate, imitate the lowly God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son of God came in the character of a man and was made low.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou art taught to become humble, not of a man to become a brute.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He, being God, became man; do thou, O man, recognize that thou art man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thy whole humility is to know thyself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore because God teaches humility, He said, “I came not to do my own will, but the will of Him that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this is the commendation of humility.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whereas pride doeth its own will, humility doeth the will of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, “Whoso cometh to me, I will not cast him out.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because I came not to do my own will, but the will of Him that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I came humble, I came to teach humility, I came a master of humility: he that cometh to me is made one body with me; he that cometh to me becomes humble; he who adhereth to me will be humble, because he doeth not his own will, but the will of God; and therefore he shall not be cast out, for when he was proud he was cast out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See those inner things commended to us in the psalm: “But the sons of men will put their trust in the covering of Thy wings.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See what it is to enter within; see what it is to flee for refuge to His protection; see what it is to run even under the Father’s lash, for He scourgeth every son whom He receiveth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But the sons of men shall put their trust under the cover of Thy wings.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is within?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They shall be filled with the plenteousness of Thy house,” when Thou shalt have sent them within, entering into the joy of their Lord; “they shall be filled with the plenteousness of Thy house; and Thou shalt give them to drink of the stream of Thy pleasure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For with Thee is the fountain of life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not away without Thee, but within with Thee, is the fountain of life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And in Thy light we shall see light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Show Thy mercy upon them that know Thee, and Thy righteousness to them that are of upright heart.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They who follow the will of their Lord, not seeking their own, but the things of the Lord Jesus Christ, they are the upright in heart, their feet shall not be moved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “God is good to Israel, to the upright in heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But, as for me, says he, my feet were almost moved.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because I was jealous at sinners, looking at the peace of sinners.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To whom is God good then, unless to the upright in heart?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For God was displeasing to me when my heart was crooked.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why displeasing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because He gave happiness to the wicked, and therefore my feet tottered, as if I had served God in vain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this reason, then, my feet were almost moved, because I was not upright of heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then is upright in heart?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Following the will of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: One man is prosperous, another man toils; the one lives wickedly and yet is prosperous, the other lives rightly and is distressed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let not him that lives rightly and is in distress be angry; he has within what the prosperous man has not: let him therefore not be saddened, nor vex himself, nor faint.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That prosperous man has gold in his own chest; this other has God in his conscience.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Compare now gold and God, chest and conscience.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The former has that which perishes, and has it where it will perish; the latter has God, who cannot perish, and has Him there whence He cannot be taken away: only if he is upright in heart; for then He enters within and goeth not out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that reason, what said he?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For with Thee is the fountain of life:” not with us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We must therefore enter within, that we may live; we must not be, as it were, content to perish, nor willing to be satisfied of our own, to be dried up, but we must put our mouth to the very fountain, where the water fails not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because Adam wished to live by his own counsel, he, too, fell through him who had fallen before through pride, who invited him to drink of the cup of his own pride.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, because “with Thee is the fountain of life, and in Thy light we shall see light,” let us drink within, let us see within.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why was there a going out thence?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear why: “Let not the foot of pride come to me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore he, to whom the foot of pride came, went out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Show that therefore he went out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And let not the hands of sinners move me;” because of the foot of pride.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why sayest thou this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They are fallen, all they that work iniquity.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where are they fallen?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In their very pride.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They were driven out, and they could not stand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, pride drove them out who were not able to stand, humility sends them in who can stand for ever.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this reason, moreover, he who said, “The bones that were brought low shall rejoice,” said before, “Thou shalt give joy and gladness to my hearing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does he mean by, “to my hearing”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By hearing Thee I am happy; because of Thy voice I am happy; by drinking within I am happy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore do I not fall; therefore “the bones that were brought low will rejoice;” therefore “the friend of the Bridegroom standeth and heareth Him;” therefore he stands, because he hears.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He drinks of the fountain within, therefore he stands.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They who willed not to drink of the fountain within, “there are they fallen: they were driven, they were not able to stand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus, the teacher of humility came not to do His own will, but the will of Him that sent Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us come to Him, enter in unto Him, be ingrafted into Him, that we may not be doing our own will, but the will of God: and He will not cast us out, because we are His members, because He willed to be our head by teaching us humility.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Finally, hear Himself discoursing: “Come unto me, ye who labor and are heavy laden: take my yoke upon you, and learn of me; for I am meek and lowly of heart:” and when ye have learned this, “ye shall find rest for your souls,” from which ye cannot be cast out; “because I am come down from heaven, not to do my own will, but the will of Him that sent me;” I teach humility; none but the humble can come unto me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Only pride casteth out; how can he go out who keeps humility and falls not away from the truth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So much as could be said about the hidden sense has now been said, brethren: this sense is hidden enough, and I know not whether I have drawn out and shaped in suitable words for you, why it is that He casteth not out him that cometh unto Him; because He came not to do His own will, but the will of Him that sent Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And this,” saith He, “is the will of the Father that sent, that of all that He hath given me I should lose nothing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that keeps humility was given to Him; the same He receives: he that keeps not humility is far from the Master of humility.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “That of all which He hath given me, I should lose nothing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “So it is not the will of your Father that one of these little ones should perish.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of the proud, there may perish; but of the little ones, none perisheth; because, “if ye will not become as this little one, ye shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Of all that the Father hath given me, I should lose nothing, but I will raise it up again on the last day.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See how here He delineates that twofold resurrection.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that cometh unto me” immediately rises again, being made humble in my members; but I will raise him up again on the last day also according to the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For this is the will of my Father that sent me, that every one who seeth the Son, and believeth on Him, may have eternal life; and I will raise him up on the last day.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said above, “Whoso heareth my word, and believeth Him that sent me:” but now, “Whoso seeth the Son, and believeth on Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has not said, seeth the Son, and believeth on the Father; for to believe on the Son is the same thing as to believe on the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because, “even as the Father hath life in Himself, so hath He given also to the Son to have life in Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That every one who seeth the Son, and believeth on Him, may have eternal life:” by believing and by passing unto life, just as by that first resurrection.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And, because that is not the only resurrection, He saith, “And I will raise him up at the last day.”
